evidence.title: White House video page capture: President Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing
evidence.source_description: Official White House video page captured during plaintiff merits investigation; local HTML saved at /aar-codex/sources/whitehouse-video.html, size 239565 bytes, sha256 0ad07b7fcb36510043a0634436628c05e934a20407a49881ee9cf1c6625be6c1.
evidence.relevance: Official White House page corroborating that the White House published a video titled 'President Trump Attends the FIFA World Cup Drawing' dated December 5, 2025; relevant to official-publication context, though not a transcript of the Ronaldo passage.

evidence.title: U.S. Soccer page extract: Final Draw scheduled start and broadcast window
evidence.source_description: Faithful text extract from the publicly accessible U.S. Soccer page 'Everything You Need to Know About the FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Draw' captured by curl during defendant merits investigation. The page HTML contained Next.js-rendered story text with the quoted paragraphs. Kennedy Center page was attempted but blocked by Cloudflare; FIFA page shell was script-rendered and did not expose readable page text through simple curl.
evidence.relevance: Shows the formal FIFA World Cup 2026 Final Draw was set for Friday, Dec. 5 at 12 p.m. ET, and distinguishes pre-draw coverage from the draw window; relevant to whether an 11:13 a.m. reporter exchange was before rather than during the draw.

evidence.title: GovInfo transcript extract: Trump reporter exchange prior to 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw
evidence.source_description: Faithful text extract from GovInfo PDF DCPD-202501162 as rendered by web extraction during plaintiff merits investigation; details cross-checked against GovInfo content-details page.
evidence.relevance: Primary official source proving the relevant speaker, date, venue/event context, and the Ronaldo utterance in remarks prior to the 2026 FIFA World Cup Draw.
